name: spotdl

metadata.name: spotdl
metadata.summary: Download your Spotify playlists and songs along with album art and metadata

documentation:
# spotDL v4 **spotDL** finds songs from Spotify playlists on YouTube and downloads them - along with album art, lyrics and metadata. [![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/spotdl/spotify-downloader?color=44CC11&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/spotDL/spotify-downloader/blob/master/LICENSE) [![PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/spotDL?color=%2344CC11&style=flat-square)](https://pypi.org/project/spotdl/) [![PyPi downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dw/spotDL?label=downloads@pypi&color=344CC11&style=flat-square)](https://pypi.org/project/spotdl/) ![Contributors](https://img.shields.io/github/contributors/spotDL/spotify-downloader?style=flat-square) [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/771628785447337985?label=discord&logo=discord&style=flat-square)](https://discord.gg/xCa23pwJWY) > spotDL: The fastest, easiest and most accurate command-line music downloader.
______________________________________________________________________ **[Read the documentation on ReadTheDocs!](https://spotdl.readthedocs.io)** ______________________________________________________________________ ## Installation Refer to our [Installation Guide](https://spotdl.rtfd.io/en/latest/installation/) for more details. ### Python (Recommended Method) - _spotDL_ can be installed by running `pip install spotdl`. - To update spotDL run `pip install --upgrade spotdl` > On some systems you might have to change `pip` to `pip3`.
Other options - Prebuilt executable - You can download the latest version from the [Releases Tab](https://github.com/spotDL/spotify-downloader/releases) - On Termux - `curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/spotDL/spotify-downloader/master/scripts/termux.sh | sh` - Arch - There is an [Arch User Repository (AUR) package](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/spotdl/) for spotDL. - Docker - Build image: ```bash docker build -t spotdl . ``` - Launch container with spotDL parameters (see section below). You need to create mapped volume to access song files ```bash docker run --rm -v $(pwd):/music spotdl download [trackUrl] ``` - Build from source ```bash git clone https://github.com/spotDL/spotify-downloader && cd spotify-downloader pip install poetry poetry install poetry run python3 scripts/build.py ``` An executable is created in `spotify-downloader/dist/`.
### Installing FFmpeg FFmpeg is required for spotDL. If using FFmpeg only for spotDL, you can simply install FFmpeg to your spotDL installation directory: `spotdl --download-ffmpeg` We recommend the above option, but if you want to install FFmpeg system-wide, follow these instructions - [Windows Tutorial](https://windowsloop.com/install-ffmpeg-windows-10/) - OSX - `brew install ffmpeg` - Linux - `sudo apt install ffmpeg` or use your distro's package manager ## Usage Using SpotDL without options:: ```sh spotdl [urls] ``` You can run _spotDL_ as a package if running it as a script doesn't work: ```sh python -m spotdl [urls] ``` General usage: ```sh spotdl [operation] [options] QUERY ``` There are different **operations** spotDL can perform. The *default* is `download`, which simply downloads the songs from YouTube and embeds metadata. The **query** for spotDL is usually a list of Spotify URLs, but for some operations like **sync**, only a single link or file is required. For a list of all **options** use ```spotdl -h```
Supported operations - `save`: Saves only the metadata from Spotify without downloading anything. - Usage: `spotdl save [query] --save-file {filename}.spotdl` - `web`: Starts a web interface instead of using the command line. However, it has limited features and only supports downloading single songs. - `url`: Get direct download link for each song from the query. - Usage: `spotdl url [query]` - `sync`: Updates directories. Compares the directory with the current state of the playlist. Newly added songs will be downloaded and removed songs will be deleted. No other songs will be downloaded and no other files will be deleted. - Usage: `spotdl sync [query] --save-file {filename}.spotdl` This create a new **sync** file, to update the directory in the future, use: `spotdl sync {filename}.spotdl` - `meta`: Updates metadata for the provided song files.
## Music Sourcing and Audio Quality spotDL uses YouTube as a source for music downloads. This method is used to avoid any issues related to downloading music from Spotify. > **Note** > Users are responsible for their actions and potential legal consequences. We do not support unauthorized downloading of copyrighted material and take no responsibility for user actions. ### Audio Quality spotDL downloads music from YouTube and is designed to always download the highest possible bitrate; which is 128 kbps for regular users and 256 kbps for YouTube Music premium users. Check the [Audio Formats](docs/usage.md#audio-formats-and-quality) page for more info. ## Contributing Interested in contributing? Check out our [CONTRIBUTING.md](docs/CONTRIBUTING.md) to find resources around contributing along with a guide on how to set up a development environment. #### Join our amazing community as a code contributor, and help accelerate

## Donate help support the development and maintenance of the software ❤️ [![paypal](https://img.shields.io/badge/paypal-%2300457C.svg?&style=for-the-badge&logo=paypal&logoColor=white)](https://paypal.me/kko7) [![kofi](https://img.shields.io/badge/kofi-%23F16061.svg?&style=for-the-badge&logo=ko-fi&logoColor=white)](https://ko-fi.com/xnetcat) ## License This project is Licensed under the [MIT](/LICENSE) License.

A column containing text.

### Arguments
- text_format: The format string to use for the text.
- style: The style to use for the text.
- justify: The justification to use for the text.
- markup: Whether or not the text should be rendered as markup.
- highlighter: A Highlighter to use for highlighting the text.
- overflow: The overflow method to use for truncating the text.
- width: The maximum width of the text.

modules.name: spotdl.download.downloader
documentation: Downloader module, this is where all the downloading pre/post processing happens etc.

classes.name: spotdl.download.downloader.DownloaderError
documentation: Base class for all exceptions related to downloaders.

classes.name: spotdl.download.downloader.Downloader

methods.name: spotdl.download.downloader.Downloader.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Downloader class.

### Arguments
- settings: The settings to use.
- loop: The event loop to use.

### Notes
- `search-query` uses the same format as `output`.
- if `audio_provider` or `lyrics_provider` is a list, then if no match is found,
    the next provider in the list will be used.

methods.name: spotdl.download.downloader.Downloader.download_song
documentation:
Download a single song.

### Arguments
- song: The song to download.

### Returns
- tuple with the song and the path to the downloaded file if successful.

methods.name: spotdl.download.downloader.Downloader.download_multiple_songs
documentation:
Download multiple songs to the temp directory.

### Arguments
- songs: The songs to download.

### Returns
- list of tuples with the song and the path to the downloaded file if successful.

methods.name: spotdl.download.downloader.Downloader.pool_download
documentation:
Run asynchronous task in a pool to make sure that all processes.

### Arguments
- song: The song to download.

### Returns
- tuple with the song and the path to the downloaded file if successful.

### Notes
- This method calls `self.search_and_download` in a new thread.

methods.name: spotdl.download.downloader.Downloader.search
documentation:
Search for a song using all available providers.

### Arguments
- song: The song to search for.

### Returns
- tuple with download url and audio provider if successful.

methods.name: spotdl.download.downloader.Downloader.search_lyrics
documentation:
Search for lyrics using all available providers.

### Arguments
- song: The song to search for.

### Returns
- lyrics if successful else None.

methods.name: spotdl.download.downloader.Downloader.search_and_download
documentation:
Search for the song and download it.

### Arguments
- song: The song to download.

### Returns
- tuple with the song and the path to the downloaded file if successful.

### Notes
- This function is synchronous.

Downloader class, this is where all the downloading pre/post processing happens etc.
It handles the downloading/moving songs, multithreading, metadata embedding etc.

modules.name: spotdl._version

modules.name: spotdl
documentation:
Init module for spotdl. This module contains the main entry point for spotdl.
And Spotdl class

classes.name: spotdl.Spotdl

methods.name: spotdl.Spotdl.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Spotdl class

### Arguments
- client_id: Spotify client id
- client_secret: Spotify client secret
- user_auth: If true, user will be prompted to authenticate
- cache_path: Path to cache directory
- no_cache: If true, no cache will be used
- headless: If true, no browser will be opened
- downloader_settings: Settings for the downloader
- loop: Event loop to use

methods.name: spotdl.Spotdl.search
documentation:
Search for songs.

### Arguments
- query: List of search queries

### Returns
- A list of Song objects

### Notes
- query can be a list of song titles, urls, uris

methods.name: spotdl.Spotdl.get_download_urls
documentation:
Get the download urls for a list of songs.

### Arguments
- songs: List of Song objects

### Returns
- A list of urls if successful.

### Notes
- This function is multi-threaded.

methods.name: spotdl.Spotdl.download
documentation:
Download and convert song to the output format.

### Arguments
- song: Song object

### Returns
- A tuple containing the song and the path to the downloaded file if successful.

methods.name: spotdl.Spotdl.download_songs
documentation:
Download and convert songs to the output format.

### Arguments
- songs: List of Song objects

### Returns
- A list of tuples containing the song and the path to the downloaded file if successful.

Spotdl class, which simplifies the process of downloading songs from Spotify.

```python
from spotdl import Spotdl

spotdl = Spotdl(client_id='your-client-id', client_secret='your-client-secret')

songs = spotdl.search(['joji - test drive',
    'https://open.spotify.com/track/4cOdK2wGLETKBW3PvgPWqT'])

results = spotdl.download_songs(songs)
song, path = spotdl.download(songs[0])
```

### Returns

### Returns

functions.name: spotdl.utils.github.create_github_url

From the given url, produce a URL that is compatible with Github's REST API.

### Arguments

functions.name: spotdl.utils.github.download_github_dir

Downloads the files and directories in repo_url.

### Arguments

- flatten: whether to flatten the directory structure

### Notes
- Modification of https://github.com/sdushantha/gitdir/blob/master/gitdir/gitdir.py

classes.name: spotdl.utils.github.RateLimitError

Module for formatting songs into strings.

documentation:
Create the song title.

### Arguments
- song_name: the name of the song
- song_artists: the list of artists of the song

### Returns

- Example: "Artist1, Artist2 - Song Name"

functions.name: spotdl.utils.formatter.create_file_name

Create the file name for the song, by replacing template variables with the actual values.

functions.name: spotdl.utils.formatter.parse_duration
documentation:
Convert string value of time (duration: "25:36:59") to a float value of seconds (92219.0)

### Arguments
- duration: the string value of time

### Returns
- the float value of seconds

functions.name: spotdl.utils.formatter.to_ms
documentation:
Convert a string to milliseconds.

### Arguments
- string: the string to convert
- precision: the number of decimals to round to
- kwargs: the keyword args to convert

### Returns
- the milliseconds

### Notes
- You can either pass a string,
- or a set of keyword args ("hour", "min", "sec", "ms") to convert.
- If "precision" is set, the result is rounded to the number of decimals given.
- From: https://gist.github.com/Hellowlol/5f8545e999259b4371c91ac223409209

functions.name: spotdl.utils.formatter.restrict_filename
documentation:
Sanitizes the filename part of a Path object. Returns modified object.

### Arguments
- pathobj: the Path object to sanitize
- strict: whether sanitization should be strict

### Returns
- the modified Path object

### Notes
- Based on the `sanitize_filename` function from yt-dlp

documentation:
Utility functions for spotdl. These functions are used in every stage of the
download process.

modules.name: spotdl.utils.metadata
documentation:
Module for embedding metadata into audio files using Mutagen.

```python
embed_metadata(
    output_file=Path("test.mp3"),
    song=song_object,
    file_format="mp3",
)
```

functions.name: spotdl.utils.metadata.embed_metadata
documentation:
Set ID3 tags for generic files (FLAC, OPUS, OGG)

### Arguments
- output_file: Path to the output file.

- id3_separator: The separator used for the id3 tags.
- skip_album_art: Boolean to skip album art embedding.

documentation:
Embed the album art in the audio file.

### Arguments
- audio_file: Audio file object.
- song: Song object.

Detect lyrics type (lrc or txt) and embed them in the audio file.

- song: Song object.
- encoding: Encoding type.

Get song metadata.

- path: Path to the song.

### Returns
- Dict of song metadata.

### Raises
- OSError: If the file is not found.
- MetadataError: If the file is not a valid audio file.

functions.name: spotdl.utils.metadata.embed_wav_file
documentation:
Embeds the song metadata into the wav file

### Arguments
- output_file: The output file path
- song: The song object
- id3_separator: The separator used for the id3 tags

functions.name: spotdl.utils.web.fix_mime_types
documentation:
Fix incorrect entries in the `mimetypes` registry.
On Windows, the Python standard library's `mimetypes` reads in
mappings from file extension to MIME type from the Windows
registry. Other applications can and do write incorrect values
to this registry, which causes `mimetypes.guess_type` to return
incorrect values, which causes spotDL to fail to render on
the frontend.
This method hard-codes the correct mappings for certain MIME
types that are known to be either used by TensorBoard or
problematic in general.

modules.name: spotdl.utils.spotify
documentation:
Module for interacting with Spotify API.
To use this module, you must have a Spotify API key and Spotify API secret.

```python
import spotdl.utils.spotify
spotify.Spotify.init(client_id, client_secret)
```

documentation:
Saves the Spotify cache to a file.

### Arguments
- cache: The cache to save.

documentation: Base class for all exceptions related to SpotifyClient.

documentation:
Call method for Singleton metaclass.

### Returns
- The instance of the SpotifyClient.

methods.name: spotdl.utils.spotify.Singleton.init
documentation:
Initializes the SpotifyClient.

### Arguments
- client_id: The client ID of the application.
- client_secret: The client secret of the application.
- auth_token: The access token to use.
- user_auth: Whether or not to use user authentication.
- cache_path: The path to the cache file.
- no_cache: Whether or not to use the cache.
- open_browser: Whether or not to open the browser.

### Returns
- The instance of the SpotifyClient.

Singleton metaclass for SpotifyClient. Ensures that SpotifyClient is not
instantiated without prior initialization. Every other instantiation of
SpotifyClient will return the same instance.

methods.name: spotdl.utils.spotify.SpotifyClient._get

Overrides the get method of the SpotifyClient.
Allows us to cache requests

This is the Spotify client meant to be used in the app.
Has to be initialized first by calling
`SpotifyClient.init(client_id, client_secret, user_auth, cache_path, no_cache, open_browser)`.

- output_file: Path to output file.
- ffmpeg: ffmpeg executable to use.
- output_format: output format.
- bitrate: constant/variable bitrate.
- ffmpeg_args: ffmpeg arguments.
- progress_handler: progress handler, has to accept an integer as argument.

### Returns

### Notes

modules.name: spotdl.providers.lyrics
documentation: Lyrics providers for spotdl.

modules.name: spotdl.providers.lyrics.musixmatch
documentation: MusixMatch lyrics provider.

classes.name: spotdl.providers.lyrics.musixmatch.MusixMatch

methods.name: spotdl.providers.lyrics.musixmatch.MusixMatch.extract_lyrics
documentation:
Extracts the lyrics from the given url.

### Arguments
- url: The url to extract the lyrics from.
- kwargs: Additional arguments.

### Returns
- The lyrics of the song or None if no lyrics were found.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.lyrics.musixmatch.MusixMatch.get_results
documentation:
Returns the results for the given song.

### Arguments
- name: The name of the song.
- artists: The artists of the song.
- kwargs: Additional arguments.

### Returns
- A dictionary with the results. (The key is the title and the value is the url.)
MusixMatch lyrics provider class.

modules.name: spotdl.providers
documentation: Different types of data providers for spotdl.

modules.name: spotdl.utils.matching
documentation: Module for all things matching related

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.debug
documentation:
Log a message with MATCH level

### Arguments
- message: message to log

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.fill_string
documentation:
Create a string with strings from `strings` list
if they are not yet present in main_string
but are present in string_to_check

### Arguments
- strings: strings to check
- main_string: string to add strings to
- string_to_check: string to check if strings are present in

### Returns
- string with strings from `strings` list

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.create_clean_string
documentation:
Create a string with strings from `words` list
if they are not yet present in `string`

### Arguments
- words: strings to check
- string: string to check if strings are present in
- sort: sort strings in list
- join_str: string to join strings with

### Returns
- string with strings from `words` list

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.sort_string
documentation:
Sort strings in list and join them with `join` string

### Arguments
- strings: strings to sort
- join: string to join strings with

### Returns
- joined sorted string

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.based_sort
documentation:
Sort strings in list based on the order of strings in `based_on` list

### Arguments
- strings: strings to sort
- based_on: strings to sort `strings` list based on

### Returns
- sorted list of strings

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.check_common_word
documentation:
Check if a word is present in a sentence

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match

### Returns
- True if word is present in sentence, False otherwise

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.check_forbidden_words
documentation:
Check if a forbidden word is present in the result name

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match

### Returns
- True if forbidden word is present in result name, False otherwise

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.create_match_strings
documentation:
Create strings based on song and result to match
fill strings with missing artists

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match

### Returns
- tuple of strings to match

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.get_best_matches
documentation:
Get best matches from a list of results

### Arguments
- results: list of results to match
- score_threshold: threshold to match results

### Returns
- list of best matches

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.calc_main_artist_match
documentation:
Check if main artist is present in list of artists

### Arguments
- main_artist: main artist to check
- artists: list of artists to check

### Returns
- True if main artist is present in list of artists, False otherwise

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.calc_artists_match
documentation:
Check if all artists are present in list of artists

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match

### Returns
- artists match percentage

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.artists_match_fixup1
documentation:
Multiple fixes to the artists score for
not verified results to improve the accuracy

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match
- score: current score

### Returns
- new score

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.artists_match_fixup2
documentation:
Multiple fixes to the artists score for
verified results to improve the accuracy

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match
- score: current score

### Returns
- new score

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.artists_match_fixup3
documentation:
Calculate match percentage based result's name
and song's title if the result has exactly one artist
and the song has more than one artist

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match
- score: current score

### Returns
- new score

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.calc_name_match
documentation:
Calculate name match percentage

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match

### Returns
- name match percentage

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.calc_time_match
documentation:
Calculate time difference between song and result

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match

### Returns
- time difference between song and result

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.calc_album_match
documentation:
Calculate album match percentage

### Arguments
- song: song to match
- result: result to match

### Returns
- album match percentage

functions.name: spotdl.utils.matching.order_results
documentation:
Order results.

### Arguments
- results: The results to order.
- song: The song to order for.
- search_query: The search query.

### Returns
- The ordered results.

I got this api url from the iOS app
needs a way of removing characters
that will screw up an url
keep url safe characters

### Returns
- A list of artist and track ids if found

modules.name: spotdl.providers.audio.piped
documentation: Piped module for downloading and searching songs.

classes.name: spotdl.providers.audio.piped.Piped

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.piped.Piped.__init__
documentation:
Pipe audio provider class

### Arguments
- output_directory: The directory to save the downloaded songs to.
- output_format: The format to save the downloaded songs in.
- cookie_file: The path to a file containing cookies to be used by YTDL.
- search_query: The query to use when searching for songs.
- filter_results: Whether to filter results.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.piped.Piped.get_results
documentation:
Get results from YouTube Music API and simplify them

### Arguments
- search_term: The search term to search for.
- kwargs: other keyword arguments passed to the `YTMusic.search` method.

### Returns
- A list of simplified results (dicts)

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.piped.Piped.get_download_metadata
documentation:
Get metadata for a download using yt-dlp.

### Arguments
- url: The url to get metadata for.

### Returns
- A dictionary containing the metadata.
YouTube Music audio provider class

classes.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProviderError
documentation: Base class for all exceptions related to audio searching/downloading.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.YTDLLogger.debug
documentation: YTDL uses this to print debug messages.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.YTDLLogger.warning
documentation: YTDL uses this to print warnings.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.YTDLLogger.error
documentation: YTDL uses this to print errors.
Custom YT-dlp logger.

classes.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProvider

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProvider.__init__
documentation:
Base class for audio providers.

### Arguments
- output_directory: The directory to save the downloaded songs to.
- output_format: The format to save the downloaded songs in.
- cookie_file: The path to a file containing cookies to be used by YTDL.
- search_query: The query to use when searching for songs.
- filter_results: Whether to filter results.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProvider.get_results
documentation:
Get results from audio provider.

### Arguments
- search_term: The search term to use.
- kwargs: Additional arguments.

### Returns
- A list of results.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProvider.get_views
documentation:
Get the number of views for a video.

### Arguments
- url: The url of the video.

### Returns
- The number of views.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProvider.search
documentation:
Search for a song and return best match.

### Arguments
- song: The song to search for.

### Returns
- The url of the best match or None if no match was found.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProvider.get_best_result
documentation:
Get the best match from the results
using views and average match

### Arguments
- results: A dictionary of results and their scores

### Returns
- The best match URL and its score

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProvider.get_download_metadata
documentation:
Get metadata for a download using yt-dlp.

### Arguments
- url: The url to get metadata for.

### Returns
- A dictionary containing the metadata.

methods.name: spotdl.providers.audio.base.AudioProvider.name
documentation:
Get the name of the provider.

### Returns
- The name of the provider.

Base class for all other providers. Provides some common functionality.
Handles the yt-dlp audio handler.
